title: Reported Speech & Communication — Reading a Reported Dialogue
objectives: Hiểu và áp dụng: reading a reported dialogue
Học 24 từ vựng theo chủ đề tuần 10
Hoàn thành 40 câu hỏi trắc nghiệm tăng dần độ khó
Bám sát mục tiêu tuần: Reported Speech & Communication
lesson: **Reported Speech.** We use reported (indirect) speech to tell someone what another person said, without quotation marks. Tenses usually shift back one step: present simple → past simple ('I am tired' → he said he *was* tired); present continuous → past continuous; present perfect and past simple → past perfect; *will → would*, *can → could*, *may → might*. Pronouns and time words also change (today → that day, tomorrow → the next day). Reported questions use statement word order, no question mark, and *if/whether* for yes/no questions: 'He asked *if* I *was* free.' Useful reporting verbs include *say*, *tell*, *ask*, *explain*, *admit*, *deny*, *suggest* and *promise*. Practise reporting a short **conversation** you had.
